title: Plagiobothrys cognatus

It belongs to the order Boraginales and the class Magnoliopsida, or dicotyledons. This species is further classified as a genus within the tribe Plagioborthreae. intro: Plagiobothrys cognatus, also known as Coastal Popcornflower, is a perennial plant native to the coastal regions of California in the United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, potentially aiding in the relief of symptoms associated with arthritis and other inflammatory conditions.
